openingQuery: Something for the home, not the wardrobe

chat.content: Something for the home, not the wardrobe

Go home, not closet: the Wabi-Sabi Bowl ($65) is the one I would pull first. It has the handmade irregularity Theo responds to, works on a table or shelf, and feels collected rather than styled. If you want a softer landing, the Washed Linen Throw ($138) brings the same lived-in texture to the room.

toolCalls.description: Semantic search constrained to home goods, excluding wardrobe
toolCalls.sql:
SELECT name, brand, price, 1 - (embedding <=> $1::vector) AS similarity
FROM product_catalog
WHERE category ILIKE '%Home%'
  AND category NOT ILIKE '%Apparel%'
  AND quantity > 0
ORDER BY embedding <=> $1::vector
LIMIT 10;
toolCalls.resultSummary: Home goods ranked with Wabi-Sabi Bowl first

sections.title: The axis
paragraphs: The turn is explicit about exclusion. The query keeps the home cluster while avoiding apparel, making the category boundary visible in the SQL.
